note: Raw facts only. Derived/formula fields (A07, A13, A17, A23, A24, A43, A63-A66) are recomputed live by the analytics engine at runtime, never read from this file at import time — this snapshot exists only to seed business_parameters with source/confidence/min/max metadata.

assumptions.source: Marketing Strategy Document
assumptions.notes: Standard large catamaran licensed for 150-170 pax as per client strategy.

assumptions.source: Goa/Kerala day-cruise operators + Malpe tidal window analysis
assumptions.notes: Morning (8AM), Afternoon (12PM), Evening/Sunset (4PM). Adjustable for demand.

assumptions.source: KSTDC St. Mary's Island ferry: 1.5hr + premium positioning assumption
assumptions.notes: Includes boarding, cruise, activities, disembarkation.

assumptions.source: Karnataka coastal tourism: Oct–Feb peak. 5 months × ~24 days/month
assumptions.notes: Oct–Feb peak season. Excludes Sundays and maintenance.

assumptions.source: Karnataka coastal: Mar–May shoulder. 3 months × ~30 days
assumptions.notes: March–May. Full ops, lower demand.

assumptions.source: Monsoon June–Sep: Karnataka coast safety closures. Limited corporate/charter only.
assumptions.notes: June–Sep monsoon. Only charter/private events possible.

assumptions.source: Sum of A04+A05+A06
assumptions.notes: Formula: Peak + Shoulder + Off-season days

assumptions.source: Market gap analysis: KSTDC ferry ₹450 + premium positioning. Goa benchmark ₹800–1500
assumptions.notes: Standard 2.5hr cruise. Positioned as 2.2x government ferry price.

assumptions.source: Goa sunset cruise benchmark: ₹1200–2000. Premium positioning.
assumptions.notes: Sunset cruise, premium experience, snacks included.

assumptions.source: Industry standard: 10% for groups 10+
assumptions.notes: Applied to groups of 10 or more. Formula: (1-discount)*base_price

assumptions.source: Goa catamaran charter: ₹20,000–35,000. Karnataka lower market → conservative
assumptions.notes: Corporate/wedding/private charter. Full 35-seat vessel.

assumptions.source: Goa/Kerala cruise operator benchmark: ₹150–300. F&B + souvenir + photography
assumptions.notes: Snacks, beverages, photo packages, merchandise average per pax

assumptions.source: 70% standard, 30% premium/sunset mix assumption
assumptions.notes: Blend of standard and premium tickets

assumptions.source: Indian marine diesel: ~₹95/litre × 12-15 litres/hr for 40-50ft catamaran
assumptions.notes: Diesel @ ₹95/litre, ~12 litres/hour engine consumption. Varies with speed.

assumptions.parameter: Annual Maintenance Cost (% of vessel value)
assumptions.source: Marine industry benchmark: 5–8% of vessel value annually
assumptions.notes: Includes scheduled maintenance, parts, antifouling, certifications.

assumptions.source: 40-50ft catamaran market price India: ₹35–75 lakhs. Conservative mid.
assumptions.notes: New build or quality used. Depreciation basis.

assumptions.source: Formula: Vessel Value × Maintenance %
assumptions.notes: Annual maintenance budget

assumptions.source: Coastal Karnataka marine officer market rate 2024
assumptions.notes: Licensed captain. Mandatory for commercial operations.

assumptions.source: Coastal Karnataka semi-skilled maritime crew market rate
assumptions.notes: 2 crew members for safety, guest handling, operations.

assumptions.source: Booking staff, kiosk staff, admin — 2 FTEs @ ₹12,500 avg
assumptions.notes: Ticketing, booking management, customer service.

assumptions.source: Marine commercial insurance India: ₹75,000–1,20,000 for this class. MicroInsure benchmark.
assumptions.notes: Third party liability, passenger accident, vessel hull insurance.

assumptions.source: Malpe Port authority fees: ₹3,000/month estimated. KSTDC jetty fees.
assumptions.notes: Jetty access, harbor fees. Subject to negotiation.

assumptions.source: Formula: Fuel/hr × Trip Duration × Trips/day × Operating Days
assumptions.notes: Total annual fuel. Assumes all trips use full fuel.

assumptions.source: Sum: Maintenance+Staff+Insurance+Port Fees
assumptions.notes: Excludes fuel (variable), excludes marketing spend.

assumptions.source: Consumables, safety equipment, cleaning per passenger
assumptions.notes: Snacks, safety vests, cleaning materials per pax.

assumptions.source: Karnataka Tourism Annual Report 2022-23; Udupi District tourism estimate (triangulated from St. Mary's Island ferry throughput)
assumptions.notes: Peak season 10,000/day × 120 days + shoulder 4,500/day × 90 days + off 1,000/day × 60 days

assumptions.source: KSTDC ferry operational data; media reports Deccan Herald 2023
assumptions.notes: KSTDC government ferry count. Most reliable proxy for marine tourism interest.

assumptions.source: MAHE official website mahe.ac.in; NAAC accreditation report 2023
assumptions.notes: Manipal campus only. 65-70% are hostellers and core target segment.

assumptions.source: Karnataka Tourism Statistics 2022-23; District Collector statements
assumptions.notes: Includes religious, beach, cultural tourism. 30-40% are beach-oriented.

assumptions.source: MakeMyTrip/Booking.com listings + Karnataka Tourism accommodation register
assumptions.notes: Excludes very small lodges. Focus: properties with concierge capability.

assumptions.source: Karnataka coastal tourism peak. Post-New Year peak.
assumptions.notes: Peak month. Index = 1.0 = baseline

assumptions.source: Peak season, slightly lower than Jan
Shoulder. Students return from holidays.
Shoulder/pre-summer. Hot weather beginning.
Pre-monsoon. College exams. Lower footfall.
Monsoon begins. Coast closure Jun-Sep.
assumptions.notes: Monsoon: ops very limited. Charter/corporate only.

assumptions.source: Peak monsoon. Near-zero operations.
Peak monsoon.
Monsoon receding. Limited ops resuming.
Post-monsoon surge. Dasara/Navratri tourism boost.
assumptions.notes: Strong rebound month.

assumptions.source: Peak season begins. Good weather.
Christmas/New Year peak. Highest footfall month.
assumptions.notes: Absolute peak. Premium pricing possible.

assumptions.source: Formula: Average of Jan–Dec seasonality indices
assumptions.notes: Used for annualized estimates

assumptions.source: Marine tourism operators: 65–75% gross margin on ticket revenue after fuel/variable costs
assumptions.notes: After fuel and variable costs. Excludes fixed opex.

assumptions.source: Tourism operator benchmark: 25–35% net margin for well-run operations
assumptions.notes: After all fixed + variable + marketing costs.

assumptions.source: India corporate tax rate for small companies (Section 115BAA) or presumptive
assumptions.notes: Applicable tax rate. Adjust for actual entity structure.

assumptions.source: India SME WACC benchmark: 12–18%. Tourism sector premium.
assumptions.notes: Used for NPV calculations and payback assessment.

assumptions.source: Digital marketing industry benchmark: 3x minimum for healthy unit economics
assumptions.notes: LTV must be 3x+ CAC for sustainable acquisition.

assumptions.source: Marine tourism: 15–25% repeat. Referral rate of 15% from Nielsen data.
assumptions.notes: % who book again within 18 months. Drives LTV.

assumptions.source: Nielsen Trust Report 2023: word-of-mouth tourism 1.5–3.5 referrals/customer
assumptions.notes: Used for organic/referral channel sizing.

assumptions.source: WordStream India Digital Marketing Benchmarks 2024; Meta Business Insights
assumptions.notes: Cost per lead (form fill/DM/click to booking inquiry). Tourism vertical.

assumptions.source: WordStream India 2024: Travel/hospitality CTR 1.5–3.5%
assumptions.notes: Click-through rate for tourism category Meta ads.

assumptions.source: Meta India tourism segment: ₹120–250 CPM (2024)
assumptions.notes: Cost per thousand impressions. Lower in off-season.

assumptions.source: Google Ads India tourism: ₹15–60/click. Mid-value keywords assumed.
assumptions.notes: Avg CPC for 'catamaran cruise Karnataka', 'boat trip udupi' etc.

assumptions.source: Google Ads India tourism: 3–7% booking conversion
assumptions.notes: % of clicks that convert to confirmed booking/inquiry.

assumptions.source: Influencer.in India Benchmark 2024: 10K–100K followers, travel niche
assumptions.notes: Single Instagram Reel from micro-influencer (10K–100K followers).

assumptions.source: Industry benchmark: micro-influencers 3–7% engagement rate
assumptions.notes: % of followers who engage (like/comment/share).

assumptions.source: India hotel concierge/travel agent: 10–15% standard. 12% used.
assumptions.notes: Commission paid to referral partners per booking.

assumptions.source: India hospitality WhatsApp booking: 15–25% conversion from engaged leads
assumptions.notes: % of WhatsApp inquiries that convert to booking.

assumptions.source: Marine tourism break-even: 65%. Target: 80%. Goa operators: 85–95% peak.
assumptions.notes: % of seats filled across all peak-season trips.

assumptions.source: Conservative shoulder target: 60% of capacity
Off-season: 30% via corporate/charter. Very conservative.
assumptions.notes: Primarily charter events, corporate outings.

assumptions.source: Formula: Fixed OPEX / (Avg Ticket × Capacity × Trips/day × Op Days)
assumptions.notes: Minimum occupancy to cover fixed costs (excl. marketing).

assumptions.source: Year 1 is base year. No growth applied.
assumptions.notes: Base year.

assumptions.source: Tourism startups: 30–50% Y1→Y2 growth with marketing investment
assumptions.notes: Second vessel / expanded marketing. Aggressive but achievable.

assumptions.source: Maturing growth: 20–30% in year 3. Market penetration deepening.
assumptions.notes: Second city/port possible by Y3.
